description: Content for every duplicate-pairs.json leftId/rightId that is not a trusted-bank id (those resolve directly from src/content/questions/ by id). 'harvest' entries are reproduced from _HARVEST as read; 'synthetic_for_calibration' entries are constructed for this corpus and never appeared in any donor material verbatim except where explicitly cloned from a named real harvest id for an exact-duplicate test.

content.subject: Numeracy
content.prompt: A school fundraiser sold 34 pies on Saturday and 28 pies on Sunday. How many pies were sold in total across the two days?
content.explanation: 34 + 28 = 62. Add the ones digits, then the tens digits, regrouping ten ones into one ten.

content.subject: Numeracy
content.prompt: A school fundraiser sold 45 stickers on Saturday and 37 stickers on Sunday. How many stickers were sold in total across the two days?
content.explanation: 45 + 37 = 82. Add the ones digits, then the tens digits, regrouping ten ones into one ten.

content.subject: Mathematics
content.prompt: True or false: 91 is a prime number.
content.explanation: 91 can be divided evenly by a number other than 1 and itself, so it is not prime.

content.subject: Mathematics
content.prompt: True or false: 97 is a prime number.
content.explanation: 97 has no factors other than 1 and itself, so it is prime.

content.subject: Mathematics
content.prompt: Which of these numbers is a prime number?
content.explanation: 59 has exactly two factors (1 and itself); the other options can each be divided evenly by a smaller number.

content.subject: Mathematics
content.prompt: Which of these numbers is a prime number?
content.explanation: 89 has exactly two factors (1 and itself); the other options can each be divided evenly by a smaller number.

content.subject: Numeracy
content.prompt: The bar chart shows books read during the holidays for four students. How many more did Priya have than Tom?

assets.title: Books read during the holidays
assets.altText: A bar chart showing books read during the holidays for four students: Priya 20, Tom 15, Sam 23, Ana 12.

content.explanation: Priya had 20 and Tom had 15. 20 − 15 = 5.

content.subject: Numeracy
content.prompt: The bar chart shows goals scored this soccer season. How many did Ana and Leo have altogether?

assets.title: Goals scored this soccer season
assets.altText: A bar chart showing goals scored this soccer season for four students: Tom 22, Sam 17, Ana 25, Leo 14.

content.explanation: Ana had 25 and Leo had 14. 25 + 14 = 39.

content.subject: English
content.prompt: What does "the wind howled like a wolf" suggest?

stimulus.title: The Empty Street
stimulus.body: The wind howled like a wolf through the empty streets, and the old sign creaked as if it might snap in two.

options.text: the wind sounded loud and eerie
a wolf was following the wind
the streets were full of wolves
the sign was made of wolf fur

content.explanation: This is figurative language, not a literal statement — it means "the wind sounded loud and eerie".

content.subject: Reading
content.skill: Identifying the main idea of a passage
content.prompt: What is this passage mainly about?

stimulus.title: Learning to Ride
stimulus.body: Sam wobbled as he pushed off down the driveway. His dad jogged alongside, holding the back of the seat, ready to catch him if he tipped. By the third afternoon, Sam noticed his dad wasn't holding on anymore — he was riding all by himself! Sam pedalled faster, grinning, all the way to the letterbox and back.

options.text: A boy learning to ride a bike without training wheels
Riding to the letterbox and back
Two siblings building a cubby house in the holidays
A boy learning to swim freestyle at his weekly lessons

content.explanation: The passage is mostly about a boy learning to ride a bike without training wheels — the other options are either a small detail from the passage or describe a different passage.

content.subject: Reading
content.skill: Identifying the main idea of a passage
content.prompt: What is this passage mainly about?

stimulus.title: A Day at the Zoo
stimulus.body: The whole class climbed off the bus and lined up outside the zoo gates. First they watched the seals being fed, then they walked through the reptile house, where a python was curled up asleep. At lunchtime everyone sat under a big fig tree and talked about which animal they liked best.

options.text: Watching seals being fed
A girl looking after the class guinea pig during the holidays
A family camping trip beside a river
A class excursion to the zoo

content.explanation: The passage is mostly about a class excursion to the zoo — the other options are either a small detail from the passage or describe a different passage.

content.subject: Mathematics
content.prompt: Look at this number pattern: 3, 8, 13, 18, __. The rule is to add 5 each time. What is the next number?

assets.title: Number pattern
assets.altText: A number line marking the points 3, 8, 13, 18.

content.explanation: Following the rule (add 5 each time): the next number after 18 is 23.

content.subject: Mathematics
content.prompt: Look at this number pattern: 5, 16, 27, 38, __. The rule is to add 11 each time. What is the next number?

assets.title: Number pattern
assets.altText: A number line marking the points 5, 16, 27, 38.

content.explanation: Following the rule (add 11 each time): the next number after 38 is 49.

content.subject: Mathematics
content.skill: Skip counting and identifying number patterns
content.prompt: Look at this number pattern: 14, 21, 28, 35, __. What is the next number in the pattern?

assets.title: Skip counting by 7s
assets.altText: A number line marking the points 14, 21, 28, 35.

content.explanation: The pattern skip-counts by 7s: each number is 7 more than the one before it, so 35 + 7 = 42.

content.explanation: The pattern skip-counts by 12s: each number is 12 more than the one before it, so 60 + 12 = 72.

content.subject: Numeracy

assets.title: Stickers
assets.altText: A table showing stickers collected by three students: Ava 7, Ben 5, Zoe 12.

content.explanation: Zoe collected 12 and Ben collected 5. 12 − 5 = 7.

content.prompt: What is the perimeter of a square with side length 8 cm?
content.explanation: Perimeter = 4 x 8 = 32 cm.

content.prompt: An adult and two children go to the cinema and buy one snack combo. What is the total cost in dollars?
content.instructions: Use the price table. Enter a number of dollars.

visuals.title: Cinema prices
visuals.altText: Table listing cinema prices: adult ticket 17 dollars, child ticket 9 dollars, snack combo 7 dollars.

content.explanation: One adult ticket costs $17, two child tickets cost 2 × $9 = $18, and the snack combo costs $7. The total is 17 + 18 + 7 = $42.

content.prompt: True or false? The perimeter of this square is 28 centimetres.
content.instructions: Use the side length shown on the shape.

visuals.title: A square garden bed
visuals.altText: A square with each side labelled 7 centimetres.

content.explanation: A square has four equal sides. The perimeter is 7 + 7 + 7 + 7 = 28 centimetres, so the statement is true.

metadata.subject: numeracy
metadata.skill: Calculating the perimeter of a square

content.prompt: Why did Mrs Nguyen most likely leave the biscuits on the doorstep?
content.instructions: Read the story, then choose one answer.

stimulus.title: The New Neighbour
stimulus.body: The moving truck had barely rumbled away when Priya noticed the old woman next door watching from her verandah. Mrs Nguyen, the letterbox said. For the first week, Priya's family was too busy unpacking boxes to say hello to anyone. Every evening, Priya's parents came home from their new jobs looking worn out, and dinner was toast more often than not. On Friday morning, Priya opened the front door and nearly stepped on a tin. Inside, under a sheet of baking paper, sat two dozen golden biscuits, still warm. There was no note, but across the fence, Mrs Nguyen's curtain twitched, and Priya was sure she could hear someone humming.

options.text: She wanted to welcome the busy new family
She had baked too many biscuits by mistake
Priya's parents had asked her for help
She was selling biscuits to the neighbourhood

content.explanation: Mrs Nguyen watched the new family arrive and saw how busy and tired they were. Leaving warm biscuits without a note, then humming behind her curtain, suggests a quiet act of welcome, not a mistake, a request or a sale.

content.prompt: According to the text and the table, which class won the Golden Bin award?
content.instructions: Read the text and use the table, then choose one answer.

stimulus.title: Recycling at Wattle Creek Primary
stimulus.body: This term, Wattle Creek Primary ran a recycling challenge. Each class collected paper and cardboard in its own bin, and the office weighed every bin at the end of the term. The rules were simple: the class that recycled the greatest mass of paper would win the Golden Bin award and an extra sports session. The results for the four senior classes are shown in the table.

options.text: 5A
5B
6A
6B

visuals.title: Paper recycled this term
visuals.altText: Table showing paper recycled: class 5A collected 26 kilograms, 5B collected 32, 6A collected 29 and 6B collected 24.

content.explanation: The text says the award goes to the class that recycled the greatest mass of paper. The table shows 5B collected 32 kilograms, more than 5A, 6A and 6B, so 5B won the Golden Bin award.

stimulus.title: Learning to Skate
stimulus.body: Ben wobbled as he pushed off across the driveway. His mum jogged alongside, holding the back of his jacket, ready to catch him if he tipped. By the third afternoon, Ben noticed his mum wasn't holding on anymore — he was skating all by himself! Ben pushed off faster, grinning, all the way to the corner shop and back.

options.text: A boy learning to skate without help
Skating to the corner shop and back
Two siblings building a cubby house in the holidays
A boy learning to swim freestyle at his weekly lessons

content.explanation: The passage is mostly about a boy learning to ride a bike without training wheels — the other options are either a small detail from the passage or describe a different passage.

content.subject: Reading
content.skill: Identifying the main idea of a passage
content.prompt: What is this passage mainly about?

stimulus.title: The Rainy Sports Day
stimulus.body: Dark clouds rolled in just as the whistle blew for the first race. Within minutes the oval was slick with rain, and the teachers herded everyone under the covered walkway. Instead of races, the students played indoor games in the hall, and by the time the sun came back out, nobody minded that the sports carnival had turned into something else entirely.

options.text: A sports carnival interrupted by rain
A boy learning to ride a bike without training wheels
A class excursion to the zoo
A family camping trip beside a river

content.explanation: The passage is mostly about a boy learning to ride a bike without training wheels — the other options are either a small detail from the passage or describe a different passage.

content.subject: Reading
content.prompt: Why did Mr Lee leave a plate of mangoes on the fence post?

stimulus.title: Over the Fence
stimulus.body: Mr Lee's mango tree had dropped more fruit than his family could eat. Each morning that week, a full plate appeared on the fence post for whichever neighbour walked past first.

options.text: He wanted to share fruit his family couldn't use
He had made too many by mistake
A neighbour had asked him for help
He was selling them to the street

content.explanation: The passage says the tree dropped more fruit than the family could eat, so the plate was for sharing.

content.subject: Reading
content.prompt: Why did Aunt Ruth knit an extra scarf every winter?

stimulus.title: The Spare Scarf
stimulus.body: Aunt Ruth always knitted one more scarf than her family needed. It hung on the hook by the door until the coldest week of the year, when it always seemed to find its way onto someone who needed it.

options.text: She wanted to have a spare ready to give away
She had made too many by mistake
A neighbour had asked her for help
She was selling scarves at the market

content.explanation: The passage implies the extra scarf is kept ready to give to whoever needs it in the coldest week.
